title: The One With Phoebes Husband

jokes.text: Oh, my God! What are you doing in here?! Mom, I gotta go! I gotta go!
jokes.setup: Monica reassuring her mom about safety, claiming it's a safe building
jokes.punchline: Immediately discovering an intruder, contradicting her safety claims
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom irony - immediate contradiction of safety claim. Well-structured with perfect timing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: This is a safe building, Mom. Really safe.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What the—

dialogue.speaker: Intruder
dialogue.line: Nobody move!

jokes.text: That's fine, you just read the paper. I'm gonna get a pot. It's not for you.
jokes.setup: Monica discovering someone in her apartment
jokes.punchline: Casually offering newspaper while threatening violence with a pot
jokes.explanation: Monica's neurotic hospitality even when threatened. The clarification 'It's not for you' is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God! There's someone in my apartment!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Here, do you want the newspaper?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Because I will hit you with this pot.

jokes.text: Read the Family Circus... enjoy the gentle comedy...
jokes.setup: Tense situation with intruder
jokes.punchline: Monica's passive-aggressive recommendation of wholesome comic strip
jokes.explanation: Monica's neurotic need to be a good hostess even to intruders. The specific comic choice adds character detail.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what? Why don't you just read Garfield? It's really funny, and it might help you relax.

jokes.text: Just tell her her husband stopped by. Her what?
jokes.setup: Casual request to pass along a message
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Phoebe has a secret husband
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure. The casual delivery makes the revelation more shocking.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Can you tell Phoebe that Duncan called?

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: Actually, it's her husband. We got married in Vegas.

jokes.text: How did you do that?
jokes.setup: Monica's shocked reaction to husband revelation
jokes.punchline: Treating Phoebe's secret marriage like a magic trick
jokes.explanation: Monica's bewilderment expressed as if witnessing sleight of hand. Absurd but character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, you're married?!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, I got married in Vegas last week.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: How did we not know about this?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I didn't want to make a big deal out of it.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So what you're telling us is... you were married, and now you're not married?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Exactly!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's like the world's saddest magic trick.

jokes.text: Of course I wouldn't approve! You were totally in love with this guy who, hello, was gay.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining she didn't tell Monica because she'd be judgmental
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately proving Phoebe right by being judgmental
jokes.explanation: Monica's complete lack of self-awareness. The 'hello' adds dismissive attitude perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I didn't tell you because you'd be judgmental.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I would not be judgmental! What did you do?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: [explains something]

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's so gross! How could you do that?!

jokes.text: See? And you thought she'd be judgmental.
jokes.setup: Monica's immediate judgmental response
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dry acknowledgment that Monica proved her point
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. Phoebe's timing and Monica's obliviousness create great dynamic.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's disgusting!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: See? See? That's exactly what I'm talking about.

jokes.text: And I saw you eat a cheeseburger.
jokes.setup: Monica listing evidence of Phoebe being heartbroken
jokes.punchline: Eating meat as proof of emotional distress for vegetarian Phoebe
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific joke. For vegetarian Phoebe, eating meat is equivalent to staying in pajamas - sign of complete breakdown.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: She's been eating meat.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe's a vegetarian.

jokes.text: The fact that the underwear out on the telephone pole is yours... from when you had sex with Bobby on the terrace!
jokes.setup: Monica asking what she hasn't told Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Phoebe revealing embarrassing public sex incident
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and visual imagery. The specificity of underwear on telephone pole adds credibility to the embarrassment.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What haven't I told you?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, there was that time you and Chandler did it in Central Park.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: How did you know about that?!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I was there. I didn't see you, but I heard some very distinctive sounds coming from behind the boathouse.

jokes.text: Kind of the same as, say... I don't know, having a third nipple!
jokes.setup: Monica claiming her secret isn't big
jokes.punchline: Revealing Chandler's third nipple as 'not big'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation in the secret-revealing game. Third nipple is absurd enough to be memorable but believable enough to work.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's not that big of a secret.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? What secret?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler has a third nipple.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's not that big!

jokes.text: You bitch!
jokes.setup: Third nipple revelation
jokes.punchline: Chandler's betrayed outburst
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction. Chandler's wounded dignity comes through clearly.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why didn't you tell me? We're supposed to be friends!

jokes.text: As opposed to your other multi-functional nipples?
jokes.setup: Chandler claiming his third nipple is useless
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic question about normal nipple functionality
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm. The word 'multi-functional' applied to nipples is perfectly absurd and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What do you mean it's useless? Do your nipples do something?

jokes.text: Joey, what did you think a nubbin was?
jokes.setup: Chandler previously called it a 'nubbin'
jokes.punchline: Implying Joey didn't know what the word meant
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's vocabulary limitations and Chandler's realization.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's not a nubbin, it's a thumb.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What's a nubbin?

jokes.text: You see something, you hear a word, I thought that's what it was.
jokes.setup: Question about what Joey thought 'nubbin' meant
jokes.punchline: Joey's simple, literal interpretation process
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - completely straightforward but hilariously simple. Shows his learning process.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I thought it was a small nub.

jokes.text: Joey was in a porno movie!
jokes.setup: Game of revealing embarrassing secrets
jokes.punchline: Chandler's nuclear option revelation about Joey
jokes.explanation: Good escalation in the secret war. Direct and shocking, fits the tit-for-tat dynamic.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, I've got a good one. Joey once cried during a dog food commercial.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That dog was really struggling, Chandler!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no, no. The real secret is... Joey once asked me how to spell 'to.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I was testing you!

jokes.text: If I'm going down, I'm taking everybody with me!
jokes.setup: Secrets being revealed
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dramatic declaration of mutual destruction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation line. Chandler's dramatic flair makes it quotable even if concept is familiar.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, we're doing this? We're really doing this? Fine. I know about Monica and Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's right. And if you tell anyone about me and Rachel, I'm telling everyone about you two.

jokes.text: They let me be the guy who fixes the copier but can't... because there's people having sex on it.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining his porno role
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and innocent role he played
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept. Joey finding the most innocent possible role in pornography is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I was the guy who came in to fix the refrigerator.

jokes.text: Pressing my third nipple opens the delivery entrance... to the magical land of Narnia.
jokes.setup: Question about third nipple's function
jokes.punchline: Chandler's elaborate fantasy reference combining body humor with literature
jokes.explanation: Exceptional joke combining absurdist body humor with sophisticated literary reference. The specificity of 'delivery entrance' makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I have a third nipple.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's like a Scarlet Letter, except it's on my chest and it's not a letter.

jokes.text: You get the best huts, and women dance naked around you.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming third nipples are signs of virility in some cultures
jokes.punchline: Specific benefits that sound made-up but delivered seriously
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to Chandler's fantasy. Ross's earnest delivery of obviously fake cultural knowledge works well.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: In fact, in some cultures, a third nipple is considered a sign of virility.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, and in this culture, it's considered weird.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, you know, it's not that uncommon. I read that one in two hundred men have a third nipple.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So you're saying there's a chance.

jokes.text: Are any of these cultures, per chance, in the Tri-State area?
jokes.setup: Ross's claim about cultures valuing third nipples
jokes.punchline: Chandler's hopeful inquiry about local availability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - practical desperation disguised as casual inquiry. Great economy and timing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Do they have those here?

jokes.text: The Ice Capades? No, the Gravel Capades.
jokes.setup: Monica asking for clarification about Capades
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's invented variation with gravel instead of ice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe absurdism. The concept of Gravel Capades is perfectly ridiculous and delivered with her characteristic confidence.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: It's like Ice Capades, but on gravel. And instead of skates, you wear regular shoes. And instead of, like, athletes, it's just people from the bus.

jokes.text: The turns aren't as fast, but when Snoopy falls? Funny.
jokes.setup: Gravel Capades concept
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's serious analysis of gravel vs ice performance differences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - treating absurd concept with serious performance analysis. The Snoopy detail makes it even more ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, so gravel is much better than ice because gravel doesn't melt. Ice melts and then you slip. But gravel, gravel just sits there being all gritty and supportive.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Plus, gravel is better for the environment. Ice is just frozen water, which is wasteful. Gravel is like, recycled earth particles that have been through a lot.

jokes.text: I see, I see. Because of the third-nipple thing.
jokes.setup: Monica asking Chandler to 'fill me up' (presumably with milk)
jokes.punchline: Chandler connecting innocent request to his third nipple revelation
jokes.explanation: Good callback joke. Chandler's paranoia about everything relating to his secret creates ongoing humor.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Fill me up.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's what she said!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, I'm sorry. It's just... you know, with the third nipple thing, I'm a little sexually confused right now.

jokes.text: Is it because she's so cold in bed?
jokes.setup: Ross revealing he and Julie aren't having sex yet
jokes.punchline: Rachel's 'helpful' suggestion with obvious ulterior motive
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's jealousy masquerading as concern. Setup for larger comedic sequence.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, maybe you're just not doing it right.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I mean, maybe if you just... I could show you.

jokes.text: Or because she's kind of bossy, makes it feel like school?
jokes.setup: Rachel's first 'helpful' suggestion about Julie
jokes.punchline: Second, more specific insult disguised as help
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Rachel's passive-aggressive 'help.' The school comparison is specific and cutting.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm just trying to be helpful, but maybe you should tell Julie that her laugh is a little... loud?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: And those jeans from 1987? Not really doing her any favors.

jokes.text: there is nothing sexier... than a man who does not want to have sex.
jokes.setup: Ross worried about sexual hesitation
jokes.punchline: Rachel's counterintuitive advice that serves her interests
jokes.explanation: Clever manipulation disguised as wisdom. Rachel's self-serving advice is both funny and character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I don't know, I'm just worried that maybe I'm not... experienced enough.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's so not true. You know what? You should totally sleep with someone else first, just to build up your confidence.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, absolutely. And you know who would be perfect for that? Me.

jokes.text: That just means it's working.
jokes.setup: Rachel's advice about making Julie wait for sex
jokes.punchline: Claiming Julie's potential infidelity would be a good sign
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Rachel's sabotage. The logic is completely backwards but delivered with conviction.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You should make her wait. Make her wait for sex.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Why would I do that?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Because if she really loves you, she'll wait. And if she doesn't... well, then at least you'll know that she's sleeping with other guys.

jokes.text: More than jewelry.
jokes.setup: Ross asking if women really want this
jokes.punchline: Rachel's emphatic confirmation with materialistic comparison
jokes.explanation: Great economy - two words that perfectly cap the sequence. Jewelry reference adds character specificity.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Women really want this?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, yes! Are you kidding? This is like Christmas morning, my birthday, and winning the lottery all rolled into one!

jokes.text: I remember when you were just King Friday in Mr. Roger's Ice Is Nice.
jokes.setup: Phoebe complimenting Duncan on his success
jokes.punchline: Reference to made-up Mr. Rogers ice show
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe invented reference. Combining Mr. Rogers with ice shows is perfectly absurd and believable coming from her.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wow, Duncan, you've really made it! I'm so proud of you!

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: Thanks, Phoebe. It's been quite a journey.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know, this reminds me of the time Mr. Rogers did that ice show. 'Won't You Be My Neighbor... On Ice!' It was beautiful.

jokes.text: Ole! What? The Matador... Ole!
jokes.setup: Duncan changing clothes
jokes.punchline: Duncan's matador costume revealed with Spanish exclamation
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that works better on screen. The Spanish exclamation helps but limited by transcript format.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: ¡Olé!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Duncan, you look amazing!

jokes.text: Sorry, you had a paleontologist on your face. But it's gone now. You're all right.
jokes.setup: Intimate moment between Julie and Ross
jokes.punchline: Julie's profession-specific pickup line
jokes.explanation: Clever profession-based pickup line. The delivery as if removing lint makes it work.

dialogue.speaker: Julie
dialogue.line: You know, being a paleontologist must be really hard. You're always digging up the past. But maybe you could focus on the present... with me?

jokes.text: He told me to get over myself and just do it. I thought about what you said and about what he said. And his way, I get to have sex tonight, so...
jokes.setup: Ross choosing between Rachel's and Joey's advice
jokes.punchline: Ross's blunt cost-benefit analysis favoring sex
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Rachel's manipulation attempt. Ross's practical decision-making is character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, let's see... Rachel's advice has been helpful, insightful, and thoughtful. Joey's advice has been... well, Joey's advice. But Joey's advice comes with benefits.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: See?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So I'm going with Joey.

jokes.text: I'm fine, if you're okay watching a video... filled with two-nippled people.
jokes.setup: Group deciding to watch Joey's porno
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-deprecating reference to his third nipple
jokes.explanation: Good callback with twist on normal phrasing. 'Two-nippled people' is absurd but fits the setup.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I have a third nipple. So if anyone's going to be in a porno, it should be me.

jokes.text: Well, you know, these movies are offensive... and degrading to women and females...
jokes.setup: Monica explaining why they shouldn't watch porn
jokes.punchline: Redundant phrasing 'women and females' showing nervousness
jokes.explanation: Monica's nervousness showing through redundant phrasing. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We shouldn't watch porn because it's degrading to women and females.

jokes.text: And the lighting's always unflattering.
jokes.setup: Monica listing problems with pornography
jokes.punchline: Practical concern about cinematography mixed with moral objections
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica line - mixing moral outrage with practical concerns. The lighting criticism is hilariously specific.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I just think there are so many problems with pornography. The exploitation, the degradation of women, the...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I mean, the lighting is terrible. Who do these people think they're fooling with those overhead fluorescents?

jokes.text: Oh. Never mind. Wait a minute. That is the craziest typing test I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Ross asking about plot in porno
jokes.punchline: Ross's innocent interpretation of sex scene as typing test
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection joke. Ross's earnest confusion about what he's seeing is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, so in this scene, they're just... typing the whole time?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Ross, no. They're not typing.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: But their fingers are moving so fast! And there's all this heavy breathing... I just assumed it was a really intense typing test.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? The guy's form is actually quite good! Very efficient keyboard technique.

jokes.text: All I say is, she'd better get the job. Looks to me like he's the one getting the job.
jokes.setup: Ross hoping the woman gets the job
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sexual double entendre about who's 'getting the job'
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual double entendre but well-executed. Chandler's timing and Ross's setup work together.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I really hope she gets the job.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I hope I get the job... if you know what I mean.

jokes.text: You know, that's bad for the paper tray.
jokes.setup: Joey's line in the porno
jokes.punchline: Practical concern about copier maintenance during sex scene
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd line. Mixing workplace safety with pornography is perfectly Joey-esque and unexpectedly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Before we start, I just want to make sure — is this copier on a maintenance contract? Because I don't want to break something and have to pay for it.

jokes.text: I'm straight.
jokes.setup: Duncan needing a divorce
jokes.punchline: Revelation that gay husband is actually straight
jokes.explanation: Perfect plot twist delivered with maximum economy. Two words that completely upend the situation.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: I need a divorce.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? Why? I thought you two were happy.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: I thought so too, but my husband just told me he's straight.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, he's straight? How did you not know that before you got married?

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: He told me he was gay!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So what, he was lying this whole time?

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: Apparently he was just going through a phase.

jokes.text: You're so smart and funny... and you throw such great Academy Award parties!
jokes.setup: Phoebe confused about Duncan being straight
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's stereotypical evidence for Duncan being gay
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phoebe's innocent stereotyping. The Academy Award parties detail is specific and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, Duncan's straight? But he's a hairdresser!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: And he has a cat named Mr. Whiskers!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: And he cried during Steel Magnolias!

jokes.text: I guess on some level I always knew I was straight.
jokes.setup: Explaining his sexuality revelation
jokes.punchline: Inverting typical coming-out language for straight revelation
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion of coming-out tropes. Using typical gay self-discovery language for straight revelation is brilliant.

jokes.text: I'd get drunk, go to a straight bar... and wake up with a woman next to me.
jokes.setup: Duncan explaining his past 'experimentation'
jokes.punchline: Inverting typical gay experimentation story with straight bars
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the inversion theme. 'Straight bar' as taboo destination is clever role reversal.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: Yeah, I experimented in college. I went to a lot of straight bars.

jokes.text: Now I know I don't have a choice. I was born this way!
jokes.setup: Duncan's sexuality journey
jokes.punchline: Using born-this-way rhetoric for heterosexuality
jokes.explanation: Perfect completion of the inversion. Using gay rights language for straight identity is absurd but consistent.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: I was born this way. I didn't choose to be straight.

jokes.text: And then one day he says, 'Oh, I'm not gay!'
jokes.setup: Phoebe's shock at Duncan's revelation
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual delivery of earth-shattering news
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. Phoebe's understated delivery of major revelation fits her unique perspective.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Duncan, that's huge! Wait, so you're saying you've been living a double life this whole time?

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: Yes, Phoebe. I couldn't tell anyone.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not even mad. That's actually kind of cool. Oh, by the way, I'm a former street person who lived in a box, so... we've all got our stuff.

jokes.text: You know, it still smells like monkey in there.
jokes.setup: Awkward moment after emotional conversation
jokes.punchline: Ross's random observation about Marcel-related odor
jokes.explanation: Awkward transition joke. Ross's need to fill silence with random observations is character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Has anyone else noticed that Marcel's cage smells like a Bangladeshi restaurant?

jokes.text: That saves us the conversation.
jokes.setup: Ross's monkey smell observation
jokes.punchline: Phoebe grateful for conversational deflection
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan acknowledgment of awkwardness. Phoebe's relief at avoiding emotional discussion is relatable.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I think there's something wrong with Marcel. He smells like... monkey.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh! Well, thank God you said that, because I thought it was me.

jokes.text: I know her pretty well. Can I go?
jokes.setup: Group interrogation of Julie
jokes.punchline: Ross's boyfriend privilege plea to escape
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor. Ross's desperate attempt to escape interrogation is relatable and well-timed.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. I'm her boyfriend. Don't I get some kind of boyfriend privilege here? Like, I should be exempt from this, right?

jokes.text: My brother's straight, so...
jokes.setup: Duncan saying his parents will be okay with him being straight
jokes.punchline: Having a straight brother as precedent for acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of role reversal theme. Needing a straight sibling for family acceptance is absurd but consistent.

dialogue.speaker: Duncan
dialogue.line: My parents will be okay with me being straight. I have a straight brother as precedent for acceptance.

jokes.text: So your brother's straight, huh?
jokes.setup: Duncan leaving after emotional conversation
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's immediate interest in available brother
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - immediately moving from heartbreak to new romantic possibility. Great timing and character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So your brother's single?

jokes.text: No, Cobb. Like cobb salad. What exactly is in a cobb salad?
jokes.setup: Julie's endless childhood stories
jokes.punchline: Tangent leading to irrelevant food question showing everyone's boredom
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing how boring conversations spiral. Monica's food focus is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Boy, that Julie's a talker, huh?
jokes.setup: Julie's endless monologue
jokes.punchline: Chandler's massive understatement
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler understatement. Perfect timing after painful conversation sequence.

dialogue.speaker: Julie
dialogue.line: And then I said to him, you know, I've always believed that communication is the key to any relationship, and so I thought maybe we could sit down and really talk about our feelings, and what we both want out of life, and where we see ourselves in five years, and whether we're compatible in terms of our life goals and our values and our sense of humor and our taste in movies and music and food and...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: She talks a lot.

jokes.text: well then, that's pretty darn hard to recover from.
jokes.setup: Ross nervous about first time with Julie
jokes.punchline: Rachel adding pressure by emphasizing consequences
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Rachel's sabotage. Making Ross more nervous serves her interests while seeming helpful.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I mean, it's not like it's just sex, Ross. It's your first time with Julie. This is a huge moment. You're gonna remember this forever. No pressure.

jokes.text: Really sort of soft at first. And then maybe brush the hair away from my face.
jokes.setup: Rachel giving Ross kissing advice
jokes.punchline: Rachel getting lost in romantic fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good build-up to major moment. Rachel's advice becoming personal fantasy creates tension and anticipation.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You just have to lean in, make eye contact, and then...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: ...he pulls you close, his hand on your face, and time just stops...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: And then he kisses you like you're the only person in the world...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, who am I kidding? I'm thinking about Ross right now, aren't I?

jokes.text: And then it's just happening.
jokes.setup: Rachel's increasingly personal 'advice'
jokes.punchline: Rachel completely lost in the moment
jokes.explanation: Good climax to romantic tension. Rachel's complete absorption in fantasy sets up the moment.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, so you're telling me you've never tried the thing where you, like, really focus on what they're saying but also think about what you're wearing?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Because I do that all the time and it's like, totally works. Also, you should definitely wear something that makes you feel confident, like really confident, like how I feel in my new Marc Jacobs dress.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, wait, what were we talking about?

jokes.text: Thanks, Rach. Good night!
jokes.setup: Intense romantic moment between Ross and Rachel
jokes.punchline: Ross casually leaving as if nothing happened
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation. Ross treating intense moment as casual advice session is brilliantly frustrating.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So... I'm just gonna go. Yeah. I'll see you later.

jokes.text: Oh, God!
jokes.setup: Ross leaving after almost-kiss
jokes.punchline: Rachel's frustrated realization
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction to comedic frustration. Sets up audience sympathy for Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: He's leaving. He's actually leaving.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: We almost kissed! We were *this close* and now he's just... going to London?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: This is so typical. Of course this would happen to me.

jokes.text: Twice!
jokes.setup: Monica observing Ross got some action
jokes.punchline: Ross's proud emphasis on quantity
jokes.explanation: Good end-of-episode button. Ross's boyish pride in sexual success is character-appropriate and economical.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wow, you got some action last night!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, I did! TWO times!

tmdb_overview: The gang is amazed to learn that Phoebe married a gay Canadian ice dancer to help him get his green card. And Ross seeks relationship advice from Rachel.
insight: Phoebe's secret husband reveal sustains 3.45 jokes per minute across escalating character chaos.
